content.problemStatement:
Write a program using `while` loop to check if the positive number NNN is prime or not.

**Note:** Prime numbers are the numbers that are divisible by only 1 and the number itself.


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain a single positive number NNN.


Output Format

Output on a single line, `YES` if NNN is prime, else `NO`.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print YES if N is prime, otherwise NO. Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 4 is divisible by 2 → not prime. 11 has no divisor other than 1 and itself → prime. 25 is divisible by 5 → not prime.
1 is not prime by definition. 100 is even and greater than 2, so it is composite.

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
NO
YES
YES
NO
YES
YES
NO

solution.approach: For each N we test primality by trial division. Numbers <=1 are not prime. 2 is the only even prime. For larger numbers we first eliminate even numbers, then check odd divisors from 3 up to sqrt(N). If any divisor divides N, it is composite; otherwise it is prime. The loop uses a while construct as required. This runs in O(sqrt(N)) time per test case, which is fast enough for N up to 10^6 and T up to 100.
solution.timeComplexity: O(sqrt(N)) per test case, O(T * sqrt(N)) total.
